page: Microsoft Azure E8s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks place the E8s_v7 in the average tier for most general compute and memory tasks, with some notable strengths. Single-threaded gzip compression reached 33.58 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 13.92 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25% of tested systems. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression registered at 52.17 GB/sec, while stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks scored 2083.98 and 8040.17 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests showed 604.93 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 81.58 GB/sec for large blocks. In database and web serving workloads, the instance achieved 14.37 million operations per second on Redis SET operations and a static web serving throughput of 15.01 GB/sec.

The lack of local storage requires reliance on external network-attached storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. However, the inclusion of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), enhances its capability for specialized vector and matrix operations. While LLM inference speeds are average for small and medium models, performance drops significantly to a weak 5.83 tokens/sec for large 70B models. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage, making it suitable for relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, supported by a substantial 480 MB L3 cache and 64.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high 8.0 GB of memory per core. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded compression performance, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications that benefit from a high memory-to-CPU ratio without requiring local NVMe disks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, making it suitable for database operations and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v7 is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized virtual machine with 64 GB RAM, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density, advanced Intel instruction sets, and strong single-threaded compression performance.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 480 MB L3 cache
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression benchmark results
Supports Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
No local storage bundled; relies on external Premium Storage
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
